source_intent.raw_intent: Case 059: I am a enterprise_compliance_lead in Latin America. Build a mirroros_analysis_prompt for audit_agent using the technical_lens. Chaos input type is artifact_type_ambiguity. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a governed artifact with explicit validation gates. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Generate a MirrorOS analysis prompt for an audit agent that processes enterprise compliance data in Latin America, using a technical lens to resolve artifact type ambiguity and produce a governed, validated output. Scope: - The prompt must handle messy input with missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. It must include explicit validation gates and produce an SR8-compilable artifact with objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract sections. Constraints: - Input may contain contradictory or incomplete information. - Output must be strictly JSON with no markdown fences. - All sections must be present and non-empty. - Validation gates must check for completeness, consistency, and compliance with SR8 schema. Success Criteria: - The generated prompt resolves artifact type ambiguity by inferring the correct type from context. - All validation gates pass before output is finalized. - The output contract includes a clear specification of the artifact type, depth tier, and runtime target. Output Contract: - artifact_type: mirroros_analysis_prompt - depth_tier: D2_PRODUCTION - runtime_target: Codex - should_block: False - blocked_reason: None - compiled_intent: Objective: string; Scope: string; Constraints: string; Success Criteria: string; Output Contract: object
